commit.message:
Fix blocking SSL call and missing initial predictive fuel suggestion (regression from #241) (#242)

* Initial plan

* Fix blocking SSL call and restore initial predictive fuel suggestion

- Use async_get_clientsession(hass) instead of creating aiohttp.ClientSession()
  directly in the event loop for the TankerKönig request in __init__.py.
  This avoids the blocking ssl.load_verify_locations() call that HA's
  util/loop.py:137 warns about, and in some HA versions can raise a
  RuntimeError that silently kills the initial corridor-station fetching.

- Pass the HA-managed session through to async_enrich_stations_with_road_detour
  and the underlying fetch_all_detour_distances_osrm / fetch_road_distances_osrm
  / fetch_road_distances_osrm_many_to_one so the same pre-warmed session is
  reused for OSRM requests (no new ClientSession() in the event loop).

- Add keyword-only session parameter to all four affected functions.  When
  session=None (default) they create and close a temporary session themselves
  for backward compatibility; when a session is supplied the caller owns the
  lifecycle (session is not closed by the callee).

Co-authored-by: northpower25 <36472486+northpower25@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: split thresholds into warning and critical levels per area

Each memory area now has two threshold levels:
- Warning: first level, typically set to Notify
- Critical: second level, typically set to Purge

ThresholdConfig struct now has: warning, warning_action, critical,
critical_action fields. The settings dialog uses a 5-column grid
(area, warning threshold + action, critical threshold + action).

The monitoring thread checks both levels independently with separate
hysteresis states. If both thresholds are crossed simultaneously and
critical triggers a purge, the warning level skips its purge to avoid
double-purging but still sends a notification if configured.

Sensible defaults:
- Memory Load: warn at 80%, critical at 95%
- Modified List: warn at 1 GB, critical at 4 GB (purge)
- Standby List: warn at 2 GB, critical at 8 GB (purge)
- Available Memory: warn below 4 GB, critical below 2 GB (purge)

commit.message:
feat: auto-purge when threshold action is set to Purge

The monitoring thread now distinguishes between Notify and Purge actions:
- Notify: sends a toast notification (existing behavior)
- Purge: sends a toast + executes the appropriate purge operation
  - Memory Load / Available Memory → purge all (working sets + modified + standby)
  - Modified List → flush modified pages
  - Standby List → purge standby list

check_threshold_above/below now return Option<ThresholdAction> instead
of directly calling toast, letting the caller handle the action type.

commit.message:
fix: trigger audio from worker action messages instead of pre-scheduling (#211)

Audio was pre-scheduled on the Web Audio API hardware clock at phase
start, while visual flashes were triggered by the worker's action
messages. The two clocks (AudioContext.currentTime vs performance.now)
could drift, causing beeps to desync from the timer.

Now both audio and visual cues are triggered from the same source —
the worker's action messages — ensuring they stay in lockstep.

- Remove schedulePhaseActions/cancelAllScheduled from usePhaseRunner
- Play sounds immediately via getSoundPlayer() in the action handler
- Remove ~130 lines of dead pre-scheduling infrastructure from sounds.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

auth: Add BearerChallengePolicy for MFA step-up challenges during subscription listing (#2231)

* auth: Add BearerChallengePolicy to handle MFA step-up challenges

The auth package creates SubscriptionClient instances to list tenants and
subscriptions, but these clients had no challenge policy on their HTTP
pipeline. When users hit an MFA step-up challenge (401 with WWW-Authenticate
header) during tenant/subscription listing, the request simply fails.

Changes:
- Create BearerChallengePolicy in auth/src/utils/BearerChallengePolicy.ts
- Wire into SubscriptionClient pipeline in AzureSubscriptionProviderBase
- Export BearerChallengePolicy and getDefaultScopeFromEndpoint
- Add @azure/core-rest-pipeline as devDependency
- Bump version to 6.0.0-alpha.6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR feedback: dep classification and retry tracking

- Move @azure/core-rest-pipeline back to regular dependencies since
  BearerChallengePolicy is exported and its .d.ts references pipeline types
- Replace HTTP header-based retry marker (x-azext-challenge-retry) with a
  WeakSet to track retried requests out-of-band, avoiding sending the
  marker over the wire

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add monitoring loop with toast notifications on threshold breach

Adds a background monitoring thread that polls memory stats every 5
seconds and compares against user-configured thresholds. When a
threshold is crossed, a native Windows toast notification is shown
via winrt-toast-reborn (WinRT ToastNotificationManager API).

Hysteresis prevents notification spam: an alert fires once when the
threshold is crossed and only re-fires after the value recovers.

New modules:
- src/toast.rs: AUMID registration + notify/alert_pressure helpers
  using winrt-toast-reborn crate
- monitor.rs: settings now Arc<Mutex<Settings>> shared with the
  monitoring thread; spawn_monitor_thread() with per-area alert state

New dependency: winrt-toast-reborn 0.3 (wraps windows crate 0.61)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement headless video recording tool and improve performance (#182)

* Add record.py: headless video recording tool for gameplay capture

Implements all core components of the recording system:
- CLI with argparse: model_path, scenario, --output-path, --count,
  --only-wins, --show-attention, --no-observation, --no-model-actions,
  --device, --fps
- VideoRecorder: OpenCV MP4 writer with temp-file strategy for
  --only-wins filtering and sequential triforce_NN.mp4 naming
- FrameComposer: 1920x1080 canvas with scaled gameplay on left,
  side panel on right (observation + action panels)
- ObservationRenderer: Pillow-based entity list, directions, booleans
  with FiraCode Nerd Font and fallback
- ActionRenderer: animated GIF sprites in 2x2 grid with directional
  probabilities, SWORD/BEAMS combination, <0.1% threshold hiding
- AttentionOverlay: JET colormap heatmap ported from game_view.py
  with bilinear upsample and 55% alpha blend
- Main recording loop with --only-wins success detection and
  --max-attempts safety limit
- GIF sprite assets copied to img/
- record.py added to pylint targets (passes at 10.00/10)

* Fix record.py: single forward pass, CUDA device support, end-to-end verified

- Eliminate duplicate model forward pass: combined get_action_and_probabilities()
  does action selection and probability computation in one inference call (~2x speedup)
- Add device transfer for obs/mask tensors so --device cuda works correctly
- Fix attention weights to move obs to model device before inference
- Remove _to_device helper by inlining into get_action_and_probabilities
- Trim module to stay under pylint 1000-line limit (997 lines)
- End-to-end verified: 1920x1080 MP4 with gameplay, observation panel, action panel,
  and attention overlay all producing correct output

* Fix video codec: replace OpenCV mp4v with ffmpeg H.264

OpenCV's mp4v (MPEG-4 Part 2) codec is not supported by many players
including VLC. Replace the OpenCV VideoWriter with an ffmpeg subprocess
pipe using libx264 for proper H.264 encoding. Also replace the one
remaining cv2.resize call with Pillow to drop the OpenCV dependency
entirely from record.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix observation panel: reorder sections, fix arrows and icons

* Revert "Fix video codec: replace OpenCV mp4v with ffmpeg H.264"

This reverts commit 53df806a5a5b27f88fe8e79f8af7ff09dabe7bf7.

* Fix end_reason: read from rewards.ending instead of state info

The end reason was always empty because it was read from
state_change.state.info['end_reason'] which doesn't exist.
The ScenarioWrapper puts it on rewards.ending instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add combat engagement problem spec: camping analysis and solutions

Documents why the agent camps (stands still swinging sword) instead of
pursuing enemies. Analyzes 6 root causes and proposes 6 solutions with
a recommended A+B+C approach: enemy proximity PBRS, distance-scaled
miss penalty, and reduced danger tile penalty.

Co-authored-by: christopherhouse <748998+christopherhouse@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix GDB pane content wiped on Ctrl-W orientation toggle

Problem:
  After pressing Ctrl-W twice (vertical → horizontal → vertical), all
  text in the GDB pane (startup messages, command history) was cleared.

  Root cause: pyte.Screen.buffer is a Python defaultdict.  Every call
  to _render_live() and _all_lines() accessed screen.buffer[r] for each
  visible row, which silently created empty StaticDefaultDict entries
  for rows that GDB had never written to.  After toggling to horizontal
  split the pane grew (e.g. 19 → 38 rows), and rendering accessed rows
  19–37, populating them as phantom empty entries.  When toggling back
  to vertical, pyte.Screen.resize() called delete_lines(19) which saw
  those phantom rows in the dict and displaced them onto rows 0–18,
  overwriting all real startup content with empty rows.

  The first fix attempt (commit f9abc2a) tried to save rows to scrollback
  before resize, but did so unconditionally, adding spurious blank lines
  to the scrollback on every shrink.

Fix:
  - Change all rendering call sites (_render_live, _all_lines,
    _GDBScreen.index) from screen.buffer[r] to screen.buffer.get(r).
    dict.get() returns None without inserting, so no phantom entries are
    created.
  - Update _row_to_text to accept None for row and render it as a blank
    line (with cursor highlight if applicable).
  - Retain the conditional scrollback-save in _init_pyte so that rows
    genuinely displaced by pyte's delete_lines (where the replacement
    slot IS in the buffer) are still preserved in scrollback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add side navigation with Reports, Automation, and Settings panels

Integrate seo-automation functionality into the Frog Automation app by
adding a persistent side navigation that switches between four panels:

- Frogtomation: wraps all existing content unchanged
- Reports: 6 placeholder cards (Page Title, Meta Description, Response
  Code, Heading Structure, Canonical Tags, Core Web Vitals)
- Automation: 4 placeholder cards (Keyword Mapping, Redirect Mapping,
  Content Gap Analysis, Structured Data Audit)
- Settings: SEO Automation settings (service account, Drive folder,
  Copilot token) plus a mirrored API Integrations section

All existing functionality is preserved. Modals remain at body level.
Mobile responsive with hamburger toggle at ≤760px. Panel state persists
via localStorage.
